title: Lesson 21 — The Spy

words.definition: kept secret or done secretively, especially because illicit
words.example: A clandestine meeting in an empty parking garage.

words.definition: not openly acknowledged or displayed; secret
words.example: Covert operations funded entirely off the books.

words.definition: attempting to avoid notice or attention; secretive
words.example: She cast a furtive glance over her shoulder.

words.definition: done secretly to avoid being noticed
words.example: He took a surreptitious photo of the document.

words.definition: behaving quietly and secretly to avoid detection
words.example: Stealthy footsteps crept along the darkened hall.
They cloaked the real plan in dull, ordinary paperwork.

words.definition: done or shown openly; not secret
words.example: Overt hostility finally replaced the quiet grudge.

words.definition: clear or obvious to the eye or mind
words.example: His anger was manifest to everyone in the room.

words.definition: done openly and unashamedly, especially of something bad
words.example: A blatant lie that no one in the room believed.

words.definition: conspicuously and outrageously bad or offensive
words.example: A flagrant violation of the rules, in plain sight.

words.definition: clearly visible; attracting notice or attention
words.example: He was conspicuous by his absence from the meeting.

words.definition: designed to impress or attract notice; showy
words.example: An ostentatious display of wealth at the gala.

story: For months the operation stayed [[clandestine|clandestine]] and [[covert|covert]]. The agent's movements were [[furtive|furtive]], her methods [[surreptitious|surreptitious]] and [[stealthy|stealthy]]; she learned to [[cloak|cloak]] every message inside ordinary paperwork. Her rival, though, preferred the [[overt|overt]] approach, making his threats [[manifest|manifest]] and his contempt [[blatant|blatant]]. His [[flagrant|flagrant]] disregard for cover made him [[conspicuous|conspicuous]] — an [[ostentatious|ostentatious]] show of power that, in the end, gave the entire ring away.
